She has to transfer at Times Square.

You have to tap your card before you go through the turnstile.
You don't have to buy a ticket -- you can tap your credit card.

You are allowed to take a bike on the subway, but not at rush hour.
Do I have to transfer, or does this train go all the way?
Which platform do I have to be on -- uptown or downtown?
So I don't have to buy a ticket -- I can just tap. Is that right?
Let me check I understood: I take the E downtown, and I transfer at 42nd. Right?
Ma'am, you can't get through there with that suitcase -- the turnstile is too narrow. Use the wide gate on the left.
Thank you. Sorry -- do I have to tap in at the gate too, or only at the turnstile?
You have to tap at the gate. Same card, same fare. And you don't have to buy a MetroCard -- if you've got a credit card, just tap that.
Let me check I understood: I don't have to buy anything, I just tap my credit card. Is that right?
That's right. Where are you headed?
Midtown -- 42nd Street. Which platform do I have to be on, uptown or downtown?
Downtown, and take the E. It's the express train, so it skips most stations -- but it does stop at 42nd. You don't have to transfer at all.
So: the E, downtown platform, no transfer. And am I allowed to take this suitcase on the train?
You are. Just don't hold the doors when you get on -- that's the one thing you really can't do around here.
Excuse me, do I have to transfer to get to Midtown?
You don't have to buy a ticket -- you can just tap your credit card.
Am I allowed to take this suitcase through the gate?
Let me check I understood: the E train, downtown platform, no transfer.

Ladies and gentlemen, may I have your attention please. This is a Manhattan-bound E train, making express stops. The next stop is Jamaica Station. Passengers who have to transfer to the AirTrain, this is your stop, and you have to leave the train here. Please remember that you don't have to tap your card again for that transfer. Your fare covers the whole trip. Because of construction work this weekend, we are running express from Jamaica to Queens Plaza. That means we can't stop at Sutphin Boulevard, and we can't stop at Kew Gardens. If you need those stations, you have to get off here and take the local train on the other platform. And one more reminder. You are not allowed to hold the doors. Holding the doors delays the train for everybody behind you. Stand clear of the closing doors, please.

Welcome back. Today, three things nobody tells you about the New York subway, and I learned all three of them the hard way. Number one. The direction matters more than the line. Every tourist checks the letter. Is it the E, is it the F, is it the six. Nobody checks whether the platform says uptown or downtown. I once rode forty blocks the wrong way, and I have to tell you, the train does not stop and turn around for you. You have to go up the stairs, cross the street, and come back down on the other side. Number two. You don't have to buy anything. Not a card, not a ticket, not an app. You just tap your credit card at the turnstile and you walk through. People still stand in line at the machine for fifteen minutes, and they don't have to. Number three. The express train is not always faster for you. It is faster for the train. If your station is a local station, the express will take you straight past it, and now you have to come back. So before you get excited about the express, you have to check whether it actually stops where you are going. And one last thing you can't do, and everybody does it anyway. Do not hold the doors. Just don't. The next train is four minutes away.
